reasoning_model_1: The user requested the text to be spoken with an 'extremely high pitch'. Model 1 (00:00-00:02) successfully executes this command. reasoning_model_2: Model 2 (00:00-00:10) completely fails the instruction. Secondly, the model fails significantly in content accuracy. Instead of saying the sentence once, it enters a hallucinated loop, repeating '救命啊，我被困在这里了' approximately five times. model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the negative/positive constraint of 'extremely high pitch' effectively, producing a high-frequency audio output that matched the script. Model 2 failed on both accounts: it did not alter the pitch (keeping it normal/low), and it suffered from a severe generation error where it repeated the target sentence multiple times in a loop.
